name: Snow Report 2026-03-02 16:13:25
report:
Good evening, Beast fans,

What a day out there. Wall-to-wall sunshine, crisp air, and wide-open terrain made for a classic late-winter bluebird at The Beast. The mountain was glowing today, and conditions held strong from first chair through last lap.

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the cold, running over 100 guns between last night and today, with 95 of them dedicated to building depth on the Superstar glacier. It’s been an impressive push, and you can see the results stacking up. Guns will thin out during a brief power curtailment this evening before ramping back up after 8:00 p.m., with continued focus on Superstar, Upper Skyelark, Killink, Mouse Trap, and base areas. As temperatures rise tomorrow and the incoming system approaches, guns are expected to come offline around midday.

Speaking of that system, we’re looking at a nice refresh moving in tomorrow starting in the early afternoon. Current forecasts are calling for 2 to 4 inches, enough to freshen things up heading into mid-week. Temperatures will climb overnight from around 12 degrees at the base this evening to near 18 by sunrise, with highs tomorrow reaching 23 at the Peak and 28 at the base. Southwest winds will run 15 to 25 mph.

Tomorrow morning will favor the groomers, with over 70 trails getting refreshed overnight. Look for prime corduroy on runs like Header, Bunny Buster, Lower Wildfire, Skyeburst, and Caper to start your day. As the afternoon snow arrives and temperatures moderate, surfaces should soften up nicely for some smooth, edgeable turns.

We remain 100 percent open and continue to lead the East with the most open terrain available anywhere in the region. From long cruisers to steeps and trees, the options are all there.

Key lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

Bluebird today. Fresh snow tomorrow afternoon. The Beast keeps delivering.

We’ll see you out there.

-Josh

 

Operating Day: 112

Lifts      

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.     

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.      

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!      

Uphill Travel      

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-02 05:36:35
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

Yesterday ended up being a bright and blue day after the clouds from the mornings’ snow showers cleared up. About an inch fell which helped our snowmakers as they continue to build depth for spring skiing on the glacier.

It’s shaping up to be another bluebird day today with tons of sunshine overhead and highs around 14 degrees at the base and 7 degrees up top. A lighter west wind in the 10 to 15 mph range will keep things feeling refreshingly crisp, the kind of bright late winter Vermont day we love. To top it off, we remain 100% open and continue to lead the East with the most open terrain available anywhere in the region.

Because of this morning’s chilly start, snow surfaces will be firmer in areas, although there will be plenty of packed powder stashes to turn on. Cruise Control has reliable snow allowing for deep turns and skiers left of Superstar has many fun sections. The bumps on Shorty are still consistently spaced and in good shape to keep the mogul skiing going. Runs on groomed trails early will be the best of the day, with prime corduroy all over, more details on that below. 

This is a classic late-season snowmaking run, where we’re taking advantage of cold temps while we still can. The crew has been hammering the trails whenever they’ve had the chance and we’re not done yet, as the guns will be running on Superstar, Upper Skyelark, Killink, Mouse Trap, and around the Snowdon base area.

Our grooming team left several trails on natural terrain while still resurfacing a long list of runs. Bear Claw and Skyeburst at Bear will be smooth today, along with classics like Bunny Buster and Caper. Head over to High Road for some corduroy if you haven’t hit that sneaky cruiser this season. Be sure to check out our Lifts & Trails Report for our full list of groomers. 

Key lifts are scheduled to start spinning today at 9:00 a.m. with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m., and the Canyon Quad joining at 10:00 a.m.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with our Easy Street, Lil' Stash, and Vale Yark Parks offering a number of fun predominantly XS and S features. Timberline also continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature.

The Stash will be closed starting today so our crew can build the course for this year's Slash & Berm Banked Slalom on March 6th-7th. Limited spots are still open for Saturday's open competition, but if you want to get as close to the action as you can, consider signing up to volunteer as a course marshal, where each completed shift will earn you a FREE Killington lift ticket voucher, as well as lunch for the day.

The skies are clear and the slopes are wide open. Get out there!

 – Josh

name: Snow Report 2026-03-01 05:50:05
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

Yesterday was a beautiful day to be out enjoying the most terrain in the East. The sun popped out for a few appearances, we had tunes blasting on the K-1 patio, and it’s easy to have fun when it’s this nice out and the Beast has every trail to choose from. 

A few early snow showers may linger this morning, but skies will gradually turn mostly sunny as the day rolls on. Highs will climb to around 20 degrees at the base and 15 degrees at the peak as they’ll fall throughout the day, with northwest winds coming in at 16 – 18 mph.

The slopes delivered packed powder and softer surfaces later reminding us that spring isn’t too far down the road. The few areas of hardpack will largely be resurfaced by our grooming crew to help keep things consistent. The bump skiing has been on point and can be found all over with spots like Shorty and Great Bear holding nice consistent mogul sections. Groomers are still the place for perfect carving as the softer surfaces allow for deeper cuts. Check out Rime, Chute, and Cascade for two early cruisers to arc and the latter to carve with speed. 

Our grooming team added Double Dipper, Solitude, Sassafras, and Outer Limits to our collection of smooth trails ready to shred. Most of Bear will be resurfaced and is a great spot to get the turns going. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for our full list of groomers. 

Our snowmaking crew began to charge up the system early this morning, so you'll see some snowguns running before we get to the afternoon. They’ll be on a big push in the coming days, stacking up snow on Superstar and everything supporting the glacier to make sure we have the longest season in the East! They’ve done a tremendous job setting us up for a great spring season—give them a shout of praise for all their hard work if you see crew members in the wild. 

Key lifts are scheduled to start spinning today at 8:00 a.m. with the Canyon and South Ridge Quads joining at 9:00 a.m.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with our Easy Street, Lil' Stash, and Vale Yark Parks offering a number of fun predominantly XS and S features. Timberline also continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature.

Today is also your last day this week to take advantage of the creative natural features in The Stash! The park will close starting tomorrow so our crew can build the course for this year's Slash & Berm Banked Slalom on March 6th-7th. Limited spots are still open for Saturday's open competition, but if you want to get as close to the action as you can, consider signing up to volunteer as a course marshal, where each completed shift will earn you a FREE Killington lift ticket voucher, as well as lunch for the day.

Finally, our Tubing Park presented by bubly will be open from 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. providing more sliding fun just a short walk from the slopes. 

Have a fantastic Sunday!

 – Evan

 

Operating Day: 110 

Lifts      

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.     

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.

If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!

name: Snow Report 2026-02-28 05:52:06
report:
Good morning, Beast fans! 

Today will have a bit of a spring feel to it, but don’t worry about a lack of snow—even as we reach the end of February, we still have 100% of our terrain open, the most in the East. Yesterday was another sunny one out there, as surfaces softened up by the afternoon and led to some playful carving. 

The weather looks to be very mild today, with base temps between the high 20s and the mid 30s all day. Seems like we’ll have a slight inversion in the morning, where temperatures at the peak could be slightly warmer than at the base, but this will only last until the late morning. The morning will also come with some stronger wind gusts, more so at higher elevations. Stay tuned to our Lifts & Trails Report if winds force us to temporarily slow down any lifts. 

Key lifts are scheduled to start spinning at 8:00 a.m. with the exception of our Bear Mountain Quad, which has a 7:30 a.m. start time. If you’re an early bird, start your day at Bear Mountain to get the first laps on the whole mountain. We’ll even treat you to a free coffee or hot chocolate in the Bear Mountain Lodge from 7:00 a.m. - 9:00 a.m. The Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad will follow up with 9:00 a.m. openings. 

Snow coverage remains great all over the mountain, even in the trees. Julio was a fun advanced option yesterday, while Twister and Squeeze Play remain a great starting point for those looking to dip their toes into the glades. If you’re looking for spring-like bump skiing & riding, head to Great Bear or Upper Wildfire. Groomed trails were still dreamy, even as slightly warmer temps began to create some softer snow on top. Upper Pipe Dream, Upper Bear Claw, and Lower Wildfire were all highlights, and they should still be lots of fun today. 

On the grooming front, we had another busy night, meaning well over half our trails are groomed for you to shred. Our team spent a good amount of time flattening the whales on Superstar, meaning steeps that got attention on Friday night (Downdraft & Double Dipper) will be left alone this evening. Nearly every option by Skyeship base got a groom, including most of Home Stretch plus Touchdown and Field Goal. Middle Ovation will also be smooth once again in the morning. 

Snowmaking is off for the time being while temps sit a little too high, but our crew will be right back at it again on Sunday, continuing to build coverage primarily on Superstar. Not many places can say they’re still making snow in March! 

In our kparks, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail and many more in the woods. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

Finally, we'll be having a special après experience this afternoon: come by the K-1 Lodge deck from 2:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. for a live set from DJ Benny Shreds! If you were a fan of our 200K Shred Day party last week, you'll love the energy on the deck. 

Another fun-filled weekend is on the way. We hope you’ll be here for it! 

 – Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-27 16:05:20
report:
Good evening, Beast fans! 

Tomorrow will have a bit of a spring feel to it, but don’t worry about a lack of snow—even as we reach the end of February, we still have 100% of our terrain open, the most in the East. It was another sunny day out there today, as surfaces softened up by the afternoon and led to some playful carving. 

The weather looks to be very mild tomorrow, with base temps between the high 20s and the mid 30s all day. Seems like we’ll have a slight inversion in the morning, where temperatures at the peak could be slightly warmer than at the base, but this will only last until the late morning. The morning will also come with some stronger wind gusts, more so at higher elevations. Stay tuned to our Lifts & Trails Report if winds force us to temporarily slow down any lifts tomorrow. 

Key lifts are scheduled to start spinning at 8:00 a.m. tomorrow morning with the exception of our Bear Mountain Quad, which has a 7:30 a.m. start time. If you’re an early bird, start your day at Bear Mountain to get the first laps on the whole mountain. We’ll even treat you to a free coffee or hot chocolate in the Bear Mountain Lodge from 7:00 a.m. - 9:00 a.m. The Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad will follow up with 9:00 a.m. openings tomorrow. 

Snow coverage remains great all over the mountain, even in the trees. Julio was a fun advanced option today, while Twister and Squeeze Play remain a great starting point for those looking to dip their toes into the glades. If you’re looking for spring-like bump skiing & riding tomorrow, head to Great Bear or Upper Wildfire. Groomed trails were still dreamy today, even as slightly warmer temps began to create some softer snow on top. Upper Pipe Dream, Upper Bear Claw, and Lower Wildfire were all highlights of today, and they should still be lots of fun tomorrow. 

On the grooming front, we’ve got another busy plan overnight, meaning we’ll be left with well over half our trails groomed by the morning. Our team will spend a good amount of time flattening the whales on Superstar, meaning steeps that got attention on Friday night (Downdraft & Double Dipper) will be left alone this evening. Nearly every option by Skyeship base will get a groom, including most of Home Stretch plus Touchdown and Field Goal. Middle Ovation will also be smooth once again in the morning. 

Snowmaking is off for the time being while temps sit a little too high, but our crew will be right back at it again on Sunday, continuing to build coverage primarily on Superstar. Not many places at all can say they’re still making snow in March! 

In our kparks, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail and many more in the woods. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

Finally, we'll be having a special après experience tomorrow afternoon: come by the K-1 Lodge deck from 2:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. for a live set from DJ Benny Shreds! If you were a fan of our 200K Shred Day party last week, you'll love the energy on the deck tomorrow. 

Another fun-filled weekend is on the way. We hope you’ll be here for it! 

- Cullen

The skies opened into a wide stretch of blue yesterday as the sun poured down on our faces, setting the tone for an all-time day on the slopes with surfaces riding fantastically from top to bottom. The bumps have been fun to maneuver on trials like Skyehawk and in the woods, Twister and Squeeze Play were soft and a blast to rip as more intermediate friendly glades. If you’re looking to get into some woods that are more open and still skiing & riding well, Ramshead is your spot. Superstar is covered in that grippy and chalky snowmaking snow that is really fun to play with. Cruise Control once again delivered everything you’d want in a trail, the perfect pitch with reliable packed powder. Check out more of Needles Eye if you haven’t been down there in a bit.

Our grooming team added some big players to the plan in Ovation, Downdraft, and Double Dipper. Head over to Reason for fresh morning corduroy or Ridge Run for steeper carving and check out our Lifts & Trails Report to see the whole list of groomers. Our snowmaking crew had the same plan and fired up the snowguns on Bear Trax and Superstar, with guns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base. They’ll be turning everything off later today as temps rise and will charge back up sometime on Sunday.

Our key lifts are scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while the South Ridge and Canyon Quads will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. The Beast remains on fire!

name: Snow Report 2026-02-26 05:49:12
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

The slopes are still skiing and riding beautifully, with packed powder waiting around just about every turn. An early round of flakes dropped an inch of fresh this yesterday morning, adding a little extra sparkle to the surface. Pair that with the solid base our snowmaking crew has been building, and the mountain is staying in prime shape. We are still sitting at 100 percent open terrain with the most skiing and riding offered in the East.

A couple of light snow showers did pass through last night, leaving just a dusting of new stuff. Today starts mostly cloudy before the sun takes over. Temps top out near 18 at the top and 24 at the base with west winds around 17 to 22 mph making things feel a little chillier.

The snow surfaces here have been some of the best, with soft packed powder that loves to grab your edge and dish out smooth carving. The visibility has improved greatly, and our snowmakers have lightened up the guns to help out with that. Cascade was super enjoyable to rip down with grabby snow on a steep trail. Full House has been a great warm up run to build up the arcs and Needle’s Eye was holding fun and powdery moguls. Head over to Bear for consistent and fun riding or to the Canyon for some more challenging options. 

Our grooming team got after 79 trails with many of the same runs involved and Outer Limits plus OL Express added in. Be sure to get on that steep ripper while it’s smooth and primed for carving. Bunny Buster will also have corduroy that can be great for a mellow ride or wall to wall carving action, it's all up to you. 

Our snowmakers lit up Lower Bear Trax and Superstar, with snowguns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base. They’ve been absolutely crushing it lately, moving the guns around strategically to make snow where the weather gives us the best window and the mountain needs it most.

Our key lifts are scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while South Ridge Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

It keeps getting better, get out there! 

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 107

Lifts     

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.    

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.     

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!     

Uphill Travel     

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.
